observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the PPE including
the identification of ongoing projects recorded under the CIP accounts
and determination of non-existing PPE or Infrastructure Projects;

b. City Engineering Office (CEO) to coordinate and submit to the City
Accounting Office the status of the CIP projects for determination
whether there are completed projects from prior years and not
transferred to the appropriate PPE accounts;

c. City Accountant to:
c.1 Take up the necessary entries to adjust the PPE and CIP accounts
based on the Certificates of Project Completion and Acceptance
submitted by the CEO; and
c.2 Drop the unreconciled lumped amounts of CIP accounts carrying
beginning balances without available details from the books of
accounts, if warranted.

2.11 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct:

a. The City Inventory Committee to fast track the completion on the conduct
of the physical count of PPE by determining the properties found at station,
missing or non-existing items and its condition, indicating whether these
are serviceable or not and submit an accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team;

b. The City Accounting Office and CGSO to:
 b.1 Take up and complete the procedures under Items 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 of
 COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
 b.2 Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included
 in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that
 the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the
 PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled.

3.8 We recommended and the City Veterinarian agreed to submit the complete
inventory report of all breeding stocks, including prevailing market prices,
to the City Accountant to facilitate the updating and accurate determination
of the Breeding Stocks account balance.

4.11 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:

a. Require the all Supply Officer to timely prepare and submit the SSMI
to the CAO for the preparation of appropriate JEVs in recognizing
assets for the supplies not yet consumed or distributed;

b. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory
count of supplies and prepare and submit the RPCI not later than July
31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester; and

c. Direct the City Accountant to reconcile the accounting records of
Inventories with the RPCI.

5.8 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to monitor expenditures
incurred at year-end and strictly adhere to the accrual basis of accounting
by preparing the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the payables.

6.10 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary
adjusting entries for the outstanding reconciling items once the
corresponding details have been properly identified, in order to present
accurate and reliable balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA and other
affected accounts.

7.10 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:

a. Ensure that all the development projects funded under the 20
percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1
dated November, 2020 to fully attain the desirable socio-economic
targets and outcomes of the City; and

b. Direct the CPDC to conduct regular monitoring on the
implementation of all barangay priority projects under the 20
percent DF to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

8.7 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to expedite the identification
of additional suitable lot as settlement site and fully implement the terms
and conditions of the MOA to attain safe and decent housing for the
homeless constituents.

9.8 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:

a. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure non-incurrence of
unnecessary delay in the procurement for the project site development;

b. Direct the City Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of the
project site development and ensure its completion on schedule to
facilitate the construction of the Super Health Center; and

c. Instruct all concerned officials and personnel to strictly comply with the
terms and conditions of the MOA regarding the Construction of the
Super Health at Barangay Bato.

10.11 We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to enforce the collection
of real property taxes from the delinquent taxpayers through continuous
conduct of aggressive tax campaigns, and to exhaust all legal remedies
provided in RA No. 7160 to help achieve collection targets and support the
City’s sustainable development.

11.8 We recommended and the CDRRM Officer agreed to review and update
annually the LDRRMFIP in coordination with the CDRRMC to
incorporate therein programs, projects, and activities to be funded from
the unexpended previous years’ QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE under the
Special Trust Fund.

12.9 We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. GAD Focal Person to identify gender issues and include only
programs, activities and projects that address the purposes of GAD
fund during the conduct of planning and budgeting; and

b. City Budget Officer to properly fund and charge the expenditures
related to GAD.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Invoke the pertinent remedies provided under RA No. 8424 as amended by RA No. 10963 and Section 17 of RA No. 7160 for revenues from facilities and services considered as governmental functions to reduce tax liability in the future tax assessments by the BIR; and
Recover the expanded withholding tax and government money payments paid to the BIR in the amount of ₱347,371.13 from the concerned Job Order workers/ Contract-based Employees.

Recommendation:
11.11	We recommended and Management is amenable to re-evaluate its existing policy, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order/Contract of Service personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the prescribed standards on labor practices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant the following remedial measures in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to fairly present the Receivables as required under PPSAS No. 1:

•To issue demand letters to the persons concerned on loans receivable; and
•To file the appropriate request for authority to write-off for the dormant accounts duly supported with certified relevant documents validating the existence of the conditions, as applicable, such as the following:

1.Death certificate issued by the Philippine Statistics Office; 

2.Proof of insolvency, 

3.Certification from the Department of Trade and Industry that the debtor has no registered business, 

4.Certification from the Securities and Exchange Commission that the   Corporation is no longer active, 

5.Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the municipality/city of last known address, Proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect the receivable and demand to liquidate the cash advances and fund transfers, such as but not limited to copies of served or returned demand letters, 

6.Certification by Legal Officer of the entity of no pending case relative to the account, certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate the claim; and

7.Other justifications, like in the case of request for write-off due to loss of documents, the circumstances of the loss should be stated in the letter of request

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to: 

a.  Submit justification why no pertinent document for reclassification of these assets was made to the office of the Municipal Account despite reported project completion; 

b.  Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts; and 

c.  Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant in the reconciliation of records to determine the status and correct balances of CIP accounts.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the above-noted deficiencies and henceforth require all payments for registrations fees for seminars, trainings, including other payments and contributions to various leagues and federations of local elective officials be supported with government prescribed official receipts or AF No. 51.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to reclassify the current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account amounting to ₱32.883 million to Current Liabilities in compliance with the provisions cited under Paragraph 80, PPSAS No. 1 to allow the users of the financial statement to quickly determine the City’s ability to meet its current or short-term obligations.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to:
a. Revisit the transactions charged to Trust Liabilities-DRRM and make the necessary adjustment to correct the net variance of ₱2.023 million; and
b. Adhere to Item 5.1.12 and Annex C of COA Circular 2012-002 in recording the transfer of unexpended Quick Response and Mitigation Funds – MOOE and subsequent charges thereof to ensure the reliability of the account and mitigate the risk of mismanagement of DRRM fund.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:
a. Direct the Project in-charge and the Project Monitoring Team to immediately inform the City Mayor in case the contractor defaults on the contract or abandoned the project so appropriate action could be undertaken to forfeit the surety bond of the contractor; and
b. Send demand letter to the contractor and exhaust all possible means to recover the advances granted. Otherwise, blacklist/suspend the contractor for willful or deliberate abandonment or non-performance of the projects.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to correct the misstated accounts with the prescribed journal entry presented as follows:
Other Receivable ₱32,379,175.10
Real Property Tax Receivable ₱32,379,175.10

We recommended and the City Treasurer agreed to enforce collection from the delinquent property owners and encourage them to pay their real property and special education taxes through continuous conduct of aggressive tax campaigns and exhaust the remedies provided in RA No. 7160 to achieve the BLGF collection targets, and realize revenues for sustainable development of the City.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Human Resource and Management Office to check the necessity of hiring consultants and ascertain whether there are no available in-house personnel with technical skills and expertise to undertake the specific work; and
b. Avoid hiring of consultants who duplicate the functions of the in-house personnel and do not provide highly technical skills.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Strictly adhere to General Principle No. 2 prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, which requires that all disbursements must be paid by check to protect government funds against unauthorized use or misappropriation; b. Ensure that Management resort to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases provided for in RA No. 9184 and its recent issuances to properly and timely respond to emergencies and provide the best value for the government funds spent; and c. Instruct the City Accountant to withhold the appropriate taxes from payments made to local suppliers for the purchase of goods and services, in accordance with the relevant BIR regulations, and ensure the timely remittance thereof.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to:
a. Ensure that the CDRRMO prepares the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, with PPAs encompassing the four thematic areas and specify projects to be charged against the unexpended balances from previous years; and
b. Instruct the City Accountant to fulfil its commitment of presenting in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in compliance to Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ascertain the accuracy of the amounts presented in the FS.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Health Officer to submit the DTRs of the BHWs and the other required supporting documents as basis for the computation of the correct amounts of AHDPs and proof of eligibility pursuant to the guidelines and conditions stated under DOH and DBM JC No. 1 s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor strictly adhere to the principle that all disbursements must be paid by check pursuant to General Principle No. 2 prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Procure the required needs of the City through public bidding as mandated under RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the RIRR as recommended by the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and duly approved by the City Mayor; and recommended that the Implementing Office or City Social Development Office submit the Distribution List or similar document as proof that the sacks of rice were completely distributed to the intended beneficiaries and an inventory report for the undistributed rice must be prepared.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to reclassify the current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account amounting to ₱30.759 million to Current Liabilities in compliance with the provisions cited under Paragraph 80, IPSAS 1 to allow the users of the financial statement to quickly determine the City’s ability to meet its current or short-term obligations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to transfer the unspent balance of LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2021 to the Trust Fund books pursuant COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the City Mayor direct all Department Heads to return all unserviceable, obsolete, no longer needed equipment to the General Service Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor discontinue the practice of paying huge amounts of disbursements by cash and adhere to the principle that all disbursements must be paid by check pursuant to General Principle No. 2 prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 to protect government funds against unauthorized use or misappropriation.

Instruct the City Accounting and GS Offices to reconcile the balances of property cards with the PPE Ledger Cards of all PPE pursuant to Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

b)	Require the GSO to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and file an application for disposal to the City Auditor/Audit Team through the City Mayor pursuant to Section 167, Rule 22 of the Rules and Regulation on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments under COA Circular No. 92-386 dated                 October 20, 1992.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is discussed in the Notes to the Financial Statements in compliance with the requirements of Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a)	Direct the City Engineer/Project In-charge to expedite the preparation of Program of Works, Plans and Designs and other documents for all unimplemented projects under the 20% DF so that the desired benefits and services from these projects are delivered to the target beneficiaries without delay; 

b)	Ensure that all planned projects under the 20% DF are optimally utilized pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the City; and

c)	Direct the CPDC to conduct regular monitoring on the implementation of all projects funded by the 20% DF so that the desired level of efficiency is achieved.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a)	Direct the City Budget Officer, City Accountant and CDRRMO to utilize the unexpended balances of LDRRMF in accordance with Item 6.5 of JMC
	No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;

b)	Direct the City DRRM Council to prepare a revised LDRRMF Plan by incorporating new expenditure items and COVID-related PPAs to support the release and utilization of the unexpended prior years LDRRMF, subject to the approval of the SP and the City Mayor; and

c)	Instruct the DRRMC to formulate a comprehensive LDRRM Plan that encompasses the four thematic areas such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the City Accountant and/or the City Treasurer:

In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;

If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks especially those checks which have been outstanding for over two years and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and

Hereafter, the two offices coordinate, monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee at least one month before the check becomes stale, of the existence of the check pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
It is recommended that the City observe and implement the stipulations in the MOA with the Province of Negros Occidental by acquiring a resettlement site for its homeless constituents.

We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and certify a monthly report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund and the LDRRM Officer to submit the report to the OCD DILG - LGOO and the Office of the LGU Auditor on or before the 15th day of the following month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular             No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue following through the titling of all its land properties to strengthen claim of ownership over subject properties and ensure that all future acquisitions of land properties be properly supported with Certificates of Title.

We recommended that Management evaluate its existing policy, if any, for the determination of wage rate of Job Order personnel and establish guidelines to adhere to the minimum standards on labor practices.

We recommended Management to:
Require department heads to submit Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for all the PAPs that will be included in the Annual Budget, not only limited to the Common-Use Supplies and Equipment;
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate all PPMPs into an indicative APP in the format approved by the GPPB, and revise the APP whenever necessary; and
Furnish COA every year with a copy of the indicative APP (including the corresponding PPMPs) as approved by the HoPE, including any approved revisions.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that contracts are fully supported with the required documents before contract signing; and
Ensure the prompt submission of copies of perfected contracts along with the required supporting documents, for timely review by the Audit Team.

We recommended Management to:
Provide justification for the following:
Charges to DRRMF that do not directly relate to DRRM activities,
Charges made prior to approval of the corresponding Budget;
Submit the following for further review:
Duly approved revision of the LDRRMFIP showing revised budget of PPAs under Mitigation Fund CY 2021,
Itemized expenditures of DRRM PPAs; and
Ensure that all charges to the DRRMF are made within and/or duly supported with an approved Investment Plan/Budget, and must be made only after such approval.

We recommended that (a) the Municipal Treasurer request a certified copy of the DV from the auditor for the issuance of new check or if not necessary the Municipal Accountant should draw an adjusting entry to correct the balances; (b) draw a JEV for the restoration of unreleased checks.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to correct the ending receivable balance and implement the updated guidelines set by the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the above-mentioned provisions and implement the updated guidelines set by the GAM for LGUs for the transactions of the succeeding years to present the income and expense account fairly in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management expedite the titling of the land properties to strengthen claim of ownership over subject properties, pursuant to Section 209 of the GAM for LGUs and Section 39(2) of the PD 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
Create the Inventory Committee based on the composition prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and undertake the preliminary activities before the actual inventory taking in consonance with the stated COA Circular; and
Instruct the Committee to undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the City following the guidelines and procedures prescribed in the cited Circular in order to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the City Agriculturist conduct inventory of all breeding stocks, submit complete inventory report thereof and provide the prevailing market price for each as basis of the City Accountant in maintaining an accurate and reliable recording of the Breeding Stocks account in the books, pursuant to Items 4.5.1 and 4.5.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and IPSAS 27.
We recommended that the City Accountant record in the books the cost of inventory of COVID-19 vaccines in the total amount of ₱2,749,955.46.

We recommended that the City continue to conduct intensive effort to promote vaccine confidence to encourage its constituents to be inoculated with COVID-19 Vaccines in order to attain the herd immunity.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Health Officer to submit the DTRs of the BHWs and the other required supporting documents as basis for the computation of the correct amounts of AHDPs and proof of eligibility pursuant to the guidelines and conditions stated under DOH and DBM JC No. 1 s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor strictly adhere to the principle that all disbursements must be paid by check pursuant to General Principle No. 2 prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 to protect government funds against unauthorized use or misappropriation.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Procure the requirement needs of the City through public bidding as mandated under RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the RIRR as recommended by the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and duly approved by the City Mayor; and
Resort to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases provided for in RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price for the City is obtained.
It was also recommended that the Implementing Office or City Social Development Office submit the Distribution List or similar document as proof that the sacks of rice were completely distributed to the intended beneficiaries and an inventory report for the undistributed rice must be prepared.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Instruct all Offices to comply with procurement procedures mandated under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Direct the City Accountant to withhold the appropriate taxes before making any payment to local suppliers for the purchase of goods and services pursuant to the cited BIR regulations and ensure that the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer:
In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks especially those checks which have been outstanding for over two years and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
Coordinate and monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the stale check at least one month before the check becomes stale, pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the City Accountant immediately prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up all valid book reconciling items at once after the bank reconciliation process is finished pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to ascertain the correctness of balances of Cash-in-Bank-LCCA.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Determine existence of records to support the unliquidated fund transfer and, in case of absence of documents, prepare its aging schedule in preparation for the request for write off; and
Recommend to the City Mayor to file a request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated fund transfers to the COA ATL and/or SA pursuant to Item 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-005 to clean-up the account Due to other NGAs for fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to reclassify the current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account amounting to ₱30,759,279.86 to Current Liabilities in compliance with the provisions cited under Paragraph 80, IPSAS 1 to allow the users of the financial statement to quickly determine the City’s ability to meet its current or short-term obligations.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjustments in the books by capitalizing the borrowing costs incurred in the construction of the qualifying assets in adherence to the requirement of                    IPSAS 5.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to require the supplier to submit an Omnibus Sworn Statement for Negotiated Procurement under Emergency Cases pursuant to the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR and GPPB Circular No. 04-2016 dated December 20, 2016.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the BAC to update its Annual Procurement Plan, prepare the Procurement Management Reports and APCPI, post to the City’s official website and submit to the GPPB the foregoing reports in consonance with GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 in order to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare monthly utilization report on BGCM fund and comply with the posting and reporting requirements set forth in Section 4.0 of DBM Local Budget Circular            No. 125, in order to promote accountability and transparency in the disbursements and utilization of the funds received.

a)	The City Mayor ascertain that the projects/ programs to be implemented by the NGO/PO or league is covered by a MOA incorporating therein the terms of reference pursuant to Item 4.5.6 of COA Circular No. 2007-001; and


b)	The City Accountant to:

a)	Direct the City Engineer and Project In-charge to submit to the Audit Team, the approved extension of contract time pursuant to the requirements of Item 10 of Annex “E” of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and 

b)	Cause the payment of liquidated damages if the extension of contract is not approved.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

Enforce strict compliance by all City Officials and Employees with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002 on the proper utilization and liquidation of Cash Advances;

Direct the City Accountant to stop granting cash advances to those officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and 
Direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer on the withholding of salaries of those City officials and employees with long overdue cash advances pursuant to item 5.1.3 of same COA Circular.

We recommended that the City Accountant: 

Evaluate payables included in the schedule of Accounts Payable to determine if they are valid obligations of the City to suppliers/contractors, City officials/employees and other payees, henceforth:

Recognize liability and record under accounts payable only when; goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

Prepare adjusting entry for payables which did not pass the recognition criteria of Accounts Payable as defined under MNGAS, Volume I and the related regulation provided under Section 157 of GAAM, Volume I, by applying the following entries: and

Debit
     Accounts Payable
        Credit	  Prior Period   Adjustments

Immediately conduct reconciliation on the two reciprocal accounts and prepare adjusting entries to eliminate the difference to have a fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.

Direct the City Engineer to facilitate submission to the Audit Team copies of perfected Contract, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed together with complete supporting documents before the implementation of every infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Officials, who sign the certifications in the disbursement vouchers to be cautious in affixing their signatures to protect government funds and to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft at the end of the year pursuant to Section 337 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the City Accountant and/or the City Treasurer:
In case requests for replacement of stale checks are filed, cancel the checks and prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustment of the affected accounts in the books and issue substitute checks following the procedures under Section 59.c of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II;
If no request is filed, immediately cancel the stale checks especially those checks which have been outstanding for over two years and prepare a JEV to record the cancellation of the stale checks; and
Hereafter, the two offices coordinate, monitor checks already issued but remained outstanding in the books and send a written notice to the payee at least one month before the check becomes stale, of the existence of the check pursuant to second paragraph of Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Enforce strict compliance by all City Officials and Employees with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002 on the proper utilization and liquidation of Cash Advances;
Direct the City Accountant to stop granting cash advances to those officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made pursuant to Item 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer on the withholding of salaries of those City officials and employees with long overdue cash advances pursuant to item 5.1.3 of same COA Circular.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Evaluate payables included in the schedule of Accounts Payable to determine if they are valid obligations of the City to suppliers/contractors, City officials/employees and other payees, henceforth:
Recognize liability and record under accounts payable only when; goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received pursuant to Section 4, Chapter 2 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Record transactions to accounts payable only when supported by sufficient evidence and when there is proper authority for its incurrence in conformity with Section 157 of GAAM, Volume 1;
Prepare adjusting entry for payables which did not pass the recognition criteria of Accounts Payable as defined under MNGAS, Volume I and the related regulation provided under Section 157 of GAAM, Volume I, by applying the following entries: and
Debit 		Accounts Payable
Credit			Prior Period Adjustments
Propose to the Sangguniang Panlungsod upon approval of the City Mayor the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund Accounts Payable, which are outstanding in the books for more than two years and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record in consonance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Immediately prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to take up in the books the additional loan proceeds from LPB-Bacolod Branch for TL No. 25, using as basis the bank credit advice to present accurate and reliable Cash-in Bank, Local Currency Current and LoansPayable accounts in the financial statements;
Direct the Accounting personnel in charge in the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement to immediately verify if there are credits to the City’s bank account per bank statement without credit advice and right away inquire from the bank through the City Accountant so that the bank could resolve the issue; and
Request for the snapshot of transactions for the preceding month of all depository accounts maintained by the City with LBP on the 1st banking day of the ensuing month so that the City Cashier could timely detect any debits and/or credits to the City’s depository accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Ascertain that debit/credit entries under the Due from Other Funds have corresponding credit/debit entries under the Due from Other Funds under the three funds maintained by the City; and
Immediately conduct reconciliation on the two reciprocal accounts and prepare adjusting entries to eliminate the difference to have a fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant to monitor the financial status of the 20% Development Fund for current and continuing appropriations;
Require the CPDC to conduct regular monitoring of the physical status of project implementation of all projects funded by the 20% DF for current as well as prior years’ continuing appropriations so the desired level of efficiency is achieved; and
Instruct the CPDC and City Engineer to implement all projects funded by the 20% Development Fund based on timelines to ensure optimum utilization of the fund so that the services and benefits from these projects are delivered to the constituents without unnecessary delay; and
Direct the CPDC to prepare status report on the implementation of projects under the 20% DF for both the current and continuing appropriations and submit quarterly to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Direct the City DRRM Council to formulate a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) plan that encompasses the four thematic areas such as disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, disaster response, disaster rehabilitation and recovery;
Also, instruct the DRRM council to stop utilizing the LDRRMF mostly for the purchase of DRRM Equipment for Disaster Response since for previous years the City had already acquired several emergency vehicles and disaster response equipment; and
Instruct the DRRM Officer to carry out LDRRMF plan and maximize the use of its available funds so that the City is prepared in terms of facilities and the communities have the capacities to respond in case calamities might occur anytime in the City.
